mainEntity.name: Can I sue my company for hearing loss or is workers comp my only option?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generally, workers' comp is your only remedy against your employer. However, you may be able to sue a third party, such as a manufacturer of defective hearing protection, for additional damages.
acceptedAnswer.description: No, you typically cannot sue your employer. Workers' compensation is an "exclusive remedy" system in Utah and Idaho. This means in exchange for getting medical bills paid regardless of who is at fault, you give up the right to sue for "pain and suffering." However, there is an exception: if a third party (like the manufacturer of defective earplugs) caused the damage, you might have a separate personal injury lawsuit against them. This is why we investigate the equipment you used. You get the speed of workers' comp for bills/wages, but we look for third-party liability to maximize your total recovery.
